title: Eczema flare follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports recording rapid worsening, pain, fever, lethargy, blistering, infection, mucosal or systemic concern only when assessed, not asserting absence or generating escalation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

investigations.text: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.
investigations.rationale: Morphology, distribution, differential, and selected investigation context were reviewed; no diagnosis or test is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

referral.text: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.
referral.rationale: The management section supports recording a clinician-entered plan, discussion, safety net, and arranged follow-up without generating skin-care, medicine, dose, or referral advice.
referral.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

follow_up.text: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.
follow_up.rationale: The UAE DHA atopic-dermatitis guideline directly supports documenting itch, dryness, flare course, morphology, distribution, atopy, sleep and quality-of-life impact during an eczema review without inferring current severity or cause.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

source_limitations: Exact body sites and morphology, trigger and exposure history, treatment and adherence, medicine and dose, response and adverse effects, infection status, diagnosis, severity, investigation, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
